mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound, the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, providing a persistent tailwind for S&P 500 prices independent of short-term macro noise. This always-on effect should manifest as a positive drift in 20-day forward returns whenever the policy rate stays below 0.5%.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window during the ZLB period, or average forward return during FEDFUNDS<0.5% periods is not significantly greater than the full-sample mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 9%, equity markets price in an approaching labor-market trough, and forward-looking investors begin front-running the eventual recovery, producing positive 60-day forward returns despite the deteriorating current-state indicator. This is a contrarian bottoming signal activated only at extreme unemployment levels.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE>9.0, the proportion of positive 60d forward returns is not significantly higher than the unconditional base rate (chi-square p>0.05), or Rank IC<0.03 over rolling 2-year windows within triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month improvement in PAYEMS and INDPRO signals a synchronized labor-and-production recovery that is more predictive of sustained equity gains than either series alone, because it confirms demand-side employment gains are matched by supply-side output expansion. Positive 20-day forward returns should be stronger when both series rise together than when only one does.
failure_condition: The joint-improvement signal's 20d forward return distribution is not statistically distinguishable from the single-series improvement case (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST from cyclical lows signals the start of a housing-led demand recovery, which historically leads broader consumption and equity market gains, but only when it occurs in the context of a post-recession bottoming regime rather than a mid-cycle slowdown; outside that regime, housing starts fluctuations carry no reliable equity signal.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST momentum fails to produce forward 60d returns above the unconditional mean during non-post-recession regimes, or Rank IC in the target regime is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS reflects strengthening consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenue expectations and produces a modest but persistent positive drift in short-horizon S&P 500 returns. This is a continuous always-on trend-following relationship not conditioned on any threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS MoM growth and 5d forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the relationship reverses in more than 40% of rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth falls below zero, signaling deflationary pressure, equity markets price in weakening pricing power and demand destruction risk, producing negative 20-day forward returns as investors discount future earnings more heavily amid deflation fears.
failure_condition: During periods of negative CPIAUCSL MoM growth, forward 20d returns are not significantly more negative than the unconditional mean (p>0.05), or Rank IC<0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero FEDFUNDS and elevated UNRATE indicates maximum monetary stimulus applied against a severely damaged labor market, a joint condition historically associated with the strongest subsequent equity rallies over quarterly horizons because markets anticipate stimulus-driven recovery outpacing the slow-moving labor data. Neither series alone captures this asymmetric payoff.
regime_hint: zero-rate stimulus with deep labor market slack
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS/UNRATE trigger condition produces 60d forward returns not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p>0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month uptick in INDPRO signals the earliest stage of an industrial production recovery, and such upticks are predictive of short-horizon equity gains only when they occur during an early-cycle recovery regime following a demand collapse; in mid- or late-cycle regimes the same signal carries no informational value for near-term returns.
failure_condition: In the early-cycle recovery regime, positive INDPRO MoM readings fail to precede above-median 5d forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC<0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.
